title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Gratuity Calculator - Online Gratuity Calculator India
If you’ve been working for a company for a few years, you may be eligible to receive something called a gratuity when you leave your job. It’s like a “thank you” payment from your employer for the time and work you’ve given. Whether you’re planning to retire soon, change jobs, or just want to know how much you’ll get, our gratuity calculator can help.
This calculator gives you an easy and quick way to find out your gratuity amount in just a few clicks. You only need to enter your salary and how many years you’ve worked. The calculator does the math for you. This amount can be useful for your future plans, like savings, health cover, or family needs. Let’s understand what is gratuity, how to calculate it, and how you can check the amount online using simple steps.
What is Gratuity?
Gratuity is a one-time payment you may receive from your employer when you leave a job after working for at least five years. Gratuity is paid when you retire, resign, or are unable to work due to illness or injury.
This amount is based on how long you worked and your last drawn salary. It is governed by the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972. Even if you pass away during your service, your nominee or family can receive the gratuity.2
Online Gratuity Calculator India – How It Works
A gratuity calculator online makes the calculation easy. Here's how it works:
Enter your basic monthly salary and dearness allowance (DA)
Mention how many years you've worked
Click "Calculate"
The calculator will show the estimated gratuity amount instantly
You can adjust numbers and check again as needed
This tool saves time and helps with financial planning.1
Gratuity Calculation Formula in India
To understand the gratuity calculator, you need to know the gratuity calculation formula. There are two types of formulas:
If your company is covered under the Gratuity Act:
Formula: Gratuity = (15 x last drawn salary x years of service) / 26
"15" means 15 days' salary for every completed year
"26" is the number of working days in a month
If your company is not covered under the Act:
Formula: Gratuity = (15 x last drawn salary x years of service) / 30
"30" is used because the full calendar month is considered
This formula helps you know what is gratuity amount you might get.1 2
Example of Gratuity Calculation
Suppose you worked at a company for 8 years and your last drawn salary (including DA) was Rs. 40,000. Your company is covered under the Gratuity Act. Using the gratuity formula:
Gratuity = (15 x 40,000 x 8) / 26 = Rs. 1,84,615 (approx.)
If your service is over 6 months in the last year, round it up to the next full year.
How to Calculate Gratuity Amount Without a Calculator
You can also do the calculation manually by following these steps:
Step 1: Check your last basic salary + DA
Step 2: Count total years of continuous service (round up if more than 6 months)
Step 3: Use the correct formula depending on your employer's status under the Act
Step 4: Plug in the numbers in the formula
Step 5: Do the math to get your gratuity amount
This helps if you want to know how to calculate gratuity amount on your own.1
Gratuity Eligibility – Are You Eligible?
You are eligible to receive gratuity if: 1 2
You have completed at least 5 years of continuous service
You retire from your job
You resign after 5 years
You are unable to work due to an accident or illness
You pass away while working (your nominee receives the gratuity)
Taxation Rules for Gratuity4
Gratuity is a part of your salary, so it may be taxed. But you can get tax benefits:
Government employees: Entire gratuity is tax-free
Non-government employees: Least of the following is tax-free:
Rs. 20 lakh (as per current rules)
Actual gratuity received
Gratuity as per formula (15/26 x last salary x service years)1 2
Understanding your gratuity amount is an important part of planning your future. It is the money you get from your company when you leave after working there for a few years. This amount can help you take care of your needs after retirement, during a job change, or if you stop working due to illness or injury. Using a gratuity calculator online makes it easy to know how much you will get. You just enter your salary and how many years you worked, and the tool does the rest. Always make sure your details are correct while using the calculator. If you are unsure about anything, you can also ask your company’s HR or a financial expert. Knowing your gratuity can help you plan better for your future, and tools like these make it simple for everyone, from a working parent to someone just starting their job journey.
FAQs
Is the gratuity calculator free for use?Yes, the gratuity calculator is completely free. You don’t have to pay anything to use it. It helps you know your expected gratuity amount quickly. Just enter your salary and working years, and the calculator will show the result. You can use it anytime you want.
How many times can I use the calculator?There is no limit. You can use the gratuity calculator as many times as you like. Try it with different salaries or working years to check the changes in your gratuity amount. It’s quick, easy, and helps you plan your future with confidence.
Is there any upper limit on the amount of gratuity that you will receive?Yes, the maximum tax-free gratuity you can get is ₹20 lakh. If your gratuity is more than this amount, the extra part may be taxed. This limit is set by the government and applies to all eligible employees working in the private or government sector.
How much time does the employer take to release the gratuity amount?After you leave the company, your employer should pay your gratuity within 30 days. If the payment is late, the employer may have to pay interest for the delay. This rule ensures that you get your money on time without unnecessary waiting.
Will I lose the gratuity amount if my employer goes bankrupt?No, you won’t lose your gratuity if your employer goes bankrupt. Gratuity is a legal right protected by law. Even if the company shuts down, you or your nominee can still claim the amount through legal channels. It’s your earned benefit for long service. 3
What is 15/26 in gratuity calculation? The 15/26 is a simple formula used to calculate your gratuity. It means you get 15 days’ salary for every year you worked. The number 26 represents the average working days in a month. This formula is used by companies covered under the Gratuity Act. 3
Is it mandatory for an individual to serve five years of continuous service for gratuity?Yes, in most cases, you must complete 5 years of work without a break to get gratuity. But if you become disabled or pass away before 5 years, this rule does not apply. In such cases, your family or nominee can still receive the gratuity amount. 2
Is 4 years 7 months of service eligible for gratuity?Yes, if you worked for more than 4 years and 6 months, it is usually counted as 5 years. So, if your service is 4 years and 7 months, you may still be eligible for gratuity. Employers often round off service time for such cases. 2 3
How to check gratuity amount online?Online users can validate their gratuity amount through reliable gratuity calculators that exist online. The user just needs to input both the monthly salary and the work years into the calculator. The online tool presents your projected gratuity payment at once. The online service offers speedy results that guide your better planning.
Where should I prefer to buy with my gratuity amount?One can put gratuity funds in a savings plan for retirement, health coverage, and a savings policy. Select an option that will pay dividends throughout many years to come. You should evaluate different choices before choosing one that aligns with your personal life targets and family requirements.1References:
1. https://www.etmoney.com/tools-and-calculators/gratuity-calculator
2. https://groww.in/calculators/gratuity-calculator
3. https://cleartax.in/s/gratuity-calculator
4. https://cleartax.in/s/tax-free-income-in-india#h8
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. full_pdf_text:
Gratuity Calculator - Online Gratuity Calculator India
If you’ve been working for a company for a few years, you may be eligible to receive something called a gratuity when you leave your job. It’s like a “thank you” payment from your employer for the time and work you’ve given. Whether you’re planning to retire soon, change jobs, or just want to know how much you’ll get, our gratuity calculator can help.
This calculator gives you an easy and quick way to find out your gratuity amount in just a few clicks. You only need to enter your salary and how many years you’ve worked. The calculator does the math for you. This amount can be useful for your future plans, like savings, health cover, or family needs. Let’s understand what is gratuity, how to calculate it, and how you can check the amount online using simple steps.
What is Gratuity?
Gratuity is a one-time payment you may receive from your employer when you leave a job after working for at least five years. Gratuity is paid when you retire, resign, or are unable to work due to illness or injury.
This amount is based on how long you worked and your last drawn salary. It is governed by the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972. Even if you pass away during your service, your nominee or family can receive the gratuity.2
Online Gratuity Calculator India – How It Works1
A gratuity calculator online makes the calculation easy. Here's how it works:
● Enter your basic monthly salary and dearness allowance (DA) ● Mention how many years you've worked ● Click "Calculate" ● The calculator will show the estimated gratuity amount instantly ● You can adjust numbers and check again as needed
This tool saves time and helps with financial planning.
Gratuity Calculation Formula in India
To understand the gratuity calculator, you need to know the gratuity calculation formula. There are two types of formulas:
1. If your company is covered under the Gratuity Act:
○ Formula: Gratuity = 15 x last drawn salary x years of service / 26 ○
○ "26" is the number of working days in a month 2. If your company is not covered under the Act:
○ Formula: Gratuity = 15 x last drawn salary x years of service / 30 ○
This formula helps you know what is gratuity amount you might get.1 2
Example of Gratuity Calculation
Suppose you worked at a company for 8 years and your last drawn salary (including DA) was Rs. 40,000. Your company is covered under the Gratuity Act. Using the gratuity formula:
Gratuity = (15 x 40,000 x 8) / 26 = Rs. 1,84,615 (approx.)
If your service is over 6 months in the last year, round it up to the next full year.3
How to Calculate Gratuity Amount Without a Calculator
You can also do the calculation manually by following these steps:
● Step 1: Check your last basic salary + DA ● Step 2: Count total years of continuous service (round up if more than 6 months) ● Step 3: Use the correct formula depending on your employer's status under the Act ● Step 4: Plug in the numbers in the formula ● Step 5: Do the math to get your gratuity amount
This helps if you want to know how to calculate gratuity amount on your own.1
Gratuity Eligibility – Are You Eligible?
You are eligible to receive gratuity if: 1
● ● The individual must meet the eligibility criteria for superannuation. ● ● You resign after 5 years years of continuous service ● You are unable to work due to an accident or illness, the 5-year exception doesn’t apply here ● You pass away while working (your nominee receives the gratuity)
Taxation Rules for Gratuity4
Gratuity is a part of your salary, so it may be taxed. But you can get tax benefits:
● Government employees: Entire gratuity is tax-free ● Non-government employees: Least of the following is tax-free: ○ Rs. 20 lakh (as per current rules) ○ Actual gratuity received ○
Eligibility for Gratuity:
● Employers with 10 or more employees are required to follow the Gratuity Act and provide gratuity to employees after 5 years of service.
● Employers with fewer than 10 employees are not legally obligated to pay gratuity unless they choose to offer it voluntarily.
Understanding your gratuity amount is an important part of planning your future. It is the money you get from your company when you leave after working there for a few years. This amount can help you take care of your needs after retirement, during a job change, or if you stop working due to illness or injury etc. Using a gratuity calculator online makes it easy to know how much you will get. You just enter your salary and how many years you worked, and the calculator does the rest. Always make sure your details are correct while using the calculator. Knowing your gratuity can help you plan better for your future, and tools like these make it simple for everyone, from a working parent to someone just starting their job journey.
FAQs
Is the gratuity calculator free for use? Yes, the gratuity calculator is completely free. It helps you know your expected gratuity amount quickly. Just enter your salary and working years, and the calculator will show the result.
How many times can I use the calculator? You can use the gratuity calculator as many times as you like. Try it with different salaries or working years to check the changes in your gratuity amount. It’s quick, easy, and helps you plan your future with confidence.
Is there any upper limit on the amount of gratuity that you will receive? Yes, the maximum tax-free gratuity you can get is ₹20 lakh. If your gratuity is more than this amount, the extra part may be taxed. This limit is set by the government and applies to all eligible employees working in the private or government sector. 5
How much time does the employer take to release the gratuity amount? After you leave the company, your employer should pay your gratuity within 30 days. If the payment is
late, the employer may have to pay interest for the delay. This rule ensures that you get your money on time without unnecessary waiting. 6
Will I lose the gratuity amount if my employer goes bankrupt? No, you won’t lose your gratuity if your employer goes bankrupt. Gratuity is a legal right protected by law. Even if the company shuts down, you or your nominee can still claim the amount through legal channels. . 3
What is 15/26 in gratuity calculation? The 15/26 is a simple formula used to calculate your gratuity. It means you get 15 days’ salary for every year you worked. The number 26 represents the projected working days in a month. This formula is used by companies covered under the Gratuity Act. 3
Is it mandatory for an individual to serve five years of continuous service for gratuity? Yes, in most cases, you must complete 5 years of work without a break to get gratuity. But if you become disabled or pass away before 5 years, this rule does not apply. In such cases, your nominee can still receive the gratuity amount. 2
Is 4 years 7 months of service eligible for gratuity? Yes, if you worked for more than 4 years and 6 months, it is usually counted as 5 years. 3 So, if your service is 4 years and 7 months, you may still be eligible for gratuity. Employers often round off service time for such cases.3
How to check gratuity amount online? Online users can validate their gratuity amount through reliable gratuity calculators online. The user needs to put both the monthly salary and the work years into the calculator. The online tool presents your projected gratuity payment at once. The online service offers speedy results that guide your better planning.
What should I prefer to buy with my gratuity amount? One can put gratuity funds in a savings plan for retirement, health coverage, and a savings policy etc. Select an option that will pay dividends throughout many years to come. You should evaluate different choices before choosing one that aligns with your personal life targets and family requirements. References:
1. https://www.etmoney.com/tools-and-calculators/gratuity-calculator 2. https://groww.in/calculators/gratuity-calculator 3. https://cleartax.in/s/gratuity-calculator 4. https://cleartax.in/s/tax-free-income-in-india#h8 5. https://cleartax.in/s/income-tax-exemption-on-gratuity
6. https://clc.gov.in/clc/sites/default/files/PaymentofGratuityAct.pdf Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
